input: Deep Learning Fundamentals: Deep learning is a subset of machine learning using multi-layered neural networks. Key concepts: "Deep" = multiple layers learning progressively complex patterns. Mimics brain structure. Requires massive data + GPUs. Applications: language models (ChatGPT), self-driving cars, facial recognition. Trade-offs: accurate with large datasets, learns from raw data, but needs huge data, computationally expensive, black-box decisions. History: 1957 Perceptron (Rosenblatt), 2012 deep CNN won ImageNet by 10.8%. Neural networks teach computers pattern recognition. Core math: scalars, vectors, matrices, tensors. Forward propagation, loss functions (MSE, cross-entropy), backpropagation, gradient descent, learning rate.
